title: Serious collectors
wikitext:
[]: Serious collectors?             Quote Post by Tolwyn » Fri Apr 26, 2013 10:35 am I m searching for a while now for collectors of EVA Merchandise. Why? I m collecting myself for a while and look for people to exchange ideas and knowledge i m currently working on my new Museum site. It s not yet finished but i d say 70% filled www.evangelion.eu If you re collecting or know others feel free to contact me

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 26, 2013 12:10 pm I've got a modest little collection: http://i.imgur.com/u6t0k.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Apr 26, 2013 1:31 pm Hey Tolwyn, Glad to see you back again, you posted about your collection a couple years ago in that post your collection thread and that was it. I've been following your FB page and posted a few times on your wall (my initials are NN), but not recently. My collection is quite substantial but nowhere as big as yours: http://evavirtualmuseum.weebly.com/

[]:             Quote Post by Tolwyn » Fri Apr 26, 2013 2:03 pm AR-99 wrote: Hey Tolwyn, Glad to see you back again, you posted about your collection a couple years ago in that post your collection thread and that was it. I've been following your FB page and posted a few times on your wall (my initials are NN), but not recently. My collection is quite substantial but nowhere as big as yours: http://evavirtualmuseum.weebly.com/

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Apr 26, 2013 2:13 pm Good stuff. I just had an update, have had to cut back and acquire with discretion. And FanimeCon is coming up in a month, I will likely get some more stuff from there so don't want to get too crazy before it happens. When I get to Austria I'll let you know, your place is mindblowing. I don't have that kind of space and also I live in earthquake country. Another person I found who has quite a substantial collection is the curator of this cel gallery on Rubberslug: http://riogi.rubberslug.com/gallery/home.asp. However he hasn't updated lately.

[]:             Quote Post by Tian » Wed May 01, 2013 11:02 am That's quite a collection I've mostly collected scale figures of the pilots. I got a few of the older Revoltechs but gave up on them as they weren't that well made. I also bought all the Robot Damashii releases so far. I really wish Bandai would make more Robot Damashii Evas...

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed May 01, 2013 1:39 pm I had seen the FB photos but holy mother. Danny Choo giving Tolwyn some due recognition: http://www.dannychoo.com/post/en/26924/Worlds+Biggest+Evangelion+Fan.html That Kaworu standee reminds me of when I saw a Rei one in Kinokuniya SF years ago for $100 but I was a poor student at the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Wed May 01, 2013 2:51 pm You must have one high paying job to afford that much Evangelion merchandize. Definitely pretty nifty.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu May 02, 2013 11:41 pm Grimmjow wrote: You must have one high paying job to afford that much Evangelion merchandize.

[]:             Quote Post by Tolwyn » Fri May 03, 2013 2:16 am Grimmjow wrote: You must have one high paying job to afford that much Evangelion merchandize.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri May 03, 2013 9:58 am Wife and children cost a lot of money too. There's a book out by Patrick Galbraith called Otaku Spaces which contains various profiles of hard core otaku in Japan. One of them is a 42 year old, single electrician from Ibaraki who's a huge Rei fan. He has an itasha van dedicated to Rei and drives down every weekend for 2 hours to get to Akihabara with a life size Rei doll riding shotgun. Spends $500 a month on goods. One of the questions he answered was what does your wife think about this? His response: "I don't have a wife or girlfriend. Rei Ayanami is my 2D wife." I was thinking we could share resources and put together a list of what we have found to be good or not so good in terms of obtaining Eva stuffs.

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Fri May 17, 2013 10:20 am AR-99 wrote: Spends $500 a month on goods. One of the questions he answered was what does your wife think about this? His response: "I don't have a wife or girlfriend. Rei Ayanami is my 2D wife." I was thinking we could share resources and put together a list of what we have found to be good or not so good in terms of obtaining Eva stuffs.

Article: Serious collectors
 is a 42 year old, single electrician from Ibaraki who's a huge Rei fan. He has an itasha van dedicated to Rei and drives down every weekend for 2 hours to get to Akihabara with a life size Rei doll riding shotgun. Spends $500 a month on goods. One of the questions he answered was what does your wife think about this? His response: "I don't have a wife or girlfriend. Rei Ayanami is my 2D wife." I was thinking we could share resources and put together a list of what we have found to be good or not so good in terms of obtaining Eva stuffs.
